title: OID Buyer Signal Pack
audience: technical buyer, DevRel editor, PKI owner, SNMP/MIB owner, or internal registry maintainer
buyer_summary: A sanitized review of 4 sanitized OID assets found 2 evidence-ready rows, 2 unresolved rows, 1 invalid values, and a 78/100 inventory quality score.

buyer_signals.signal: 2 unresolved OID rows need owner review or registry reconciliation.
buyer_signals.why_it_matters: Unresolved identifiers make certificate policy, MIB, monitoring, or internal registry evidence harder to trust during incidents and audits.

buyer_signals.signal: 0 unknown private enterprise OIDs need PEN owner mapping.
buyer_signals.why_it_matters: Unknown enterprise arcs can hide vendor ownership gaps, stale documentation, or namespace drift.

buyer_signals.signal: 1 malformed OID values need correction before they can become evidence.
buyer_signals.why_it_matters: Bad syntax is a fast, bounded cleanup item that can be accepted through a re-run check.

buyer_signals.signal: Public directory coverage score is 1/100 for the current PEN-to-OID-base comparison.
buyer_signals.why_it_matters: Low public coverage creates a concrete reconciliation queue while keeping copied page bodies out of the published package.

first_scope_offer: Run a small sanitized OID inventory assessment: classify each row, preserve public registry evidence, identify unresolved owners, and return a remediation board plus re-run checks.
qualifying_questions: Is the OID list primarily from SNMP/MIB files, certificate policy metadata, monitoring integrations, or an internal registry export?
Can the first sample be shared as CSV or TSV with an `oid` column and sanitized asset labels?
Which unresolved rows should go back to a vendor owner, PKI owner, platform owner, or internal registry maintainer?
Would a remediation CSV, one-page decision summary, or first-call kickoff note be the easiest review format?
subject_lines: Sanitized OID inventory review with public registry evidence
Small OID cleanup assessment for PKI, MIB, or internal registry teams
Turn unresolved OID rows into an owner-ready remediation board
OID evidence pack: malformed values, owner gaps, and re-run checks
proof_points: P1: Review unmatched valid OIDs against internal registries (1)
P2: Preserve evidence-ready public registry mappings (2)
boundary_note: The public package uses sitemap metadata and open registry summaries; full OID-base page-body collection remains gated on explicit source-owner authorization.

proof_links.purpose: Sanitized assessment summary and action plan
Initial reply, intake request, and first-call agenda
